findings.summary: Top-level hierarchical schematic contains 158 total components across 8 parsed sheet instances (5 unique sheet files, dut.kicad_sch instantiated 4 times for DUT1-DUT4)
findings.detail: Component types: 65 capacitors, 27 resistors, 24 connectors, 22 ICs, 4 crystals, 4 transistors, 4 LEDs, 4 mounting holes, 3 fiducials, 1 test point. Power rails: +3V3, GND, VDC, VIO, VUSB.

findings.summary: Four crystal oscillator circuits detected (Y1, Y2, Y3, Y4), one per DUT slot, all with 27pF load caps giving 16.5pF effective load
findings.detail: Each crystal is Crystal_Small value with two 27pF load capacitors. Effective load CL_eff = (27*27)/(27+27) + 3pF stray = 16.5pF. Frequency not parseable from symbol value alone.

findings.summary: PAC1954x-x4MX four-channel power monitor (U1 in power sheet) not detected as current_sense despite having four R_Shunt resistors (R11-R14) on DUT power rails
findings.detail: The power.kicad_sch contains PAC1954x-x4MX with shunt resistors R11-R14 of type R_Shunt, but the current_sense detector returns empty. The analyzer does not recognize PAC195x-family parts or R_Shunt component type as a current sensing topology. TPS22992SRXNR load switches (IC1-IC4) correctly identified in power domain but not as regulators (expected: they are load switches, not LDOs).

findings.summary: Bus signals include JTAG/SWD per-DUT signals (DUT1_TCK/SWCLK, DUT2_TMS/SWDIO, etc.) and analog measurement nets (AN0-AN19, P200-P915) correctly classified as clock/debug/signal
findings.detail: Net classifier correctly labels TCK{slash}SWCLK as clock, TMS{slash}SWDIO as debug, SPI_SCK0 as clock, I2C_SDA2 as data. The AN0-AN19 prefix group reports width 72 with 11 missing members — this inflated width is an artifact of the numeric range spanning non-contiguous nets, not an error.

findings.summary: Four BSS138 N-channel MOSFETs (Q1-Q4) correctly detected as open-drain reset drivers, one per DUT slot, all driven by TXU0102 level translators
findings.detail: Each BSS138 has drain on RESET net, source on GND, gate driven by TXU0102QDCURQ1. No gate resistors or pulldowns detected. load_type correctly identified as connector. No flyback diodes (appropriate for signal switching, not inductive loads).

findings.summary: 100x155mm 4-layer board, fully routed (0 unrouted nets), all 158 components on front side only, 523 vias
findings.detail: Layers: F.Cu, B.Cu, In1.Cu, In2.Cu. GND plane on In1.Cu and B.Cu, VIO plane on In2.Cu. VDC zone on F.Cu with high via density (299/cm2). 7 courtyard overlaps detected — mostly connector pairs placed edge-to-edge intentionally for board-level connectors (J12/J9, JP1/J17). Board exceeds JLCPCB standard 100x100mm tier.

findings.summary: Six edge clearance warnings: two mounting holes (H2, H4) at 0.16mm from board edge, connectors J11/J14/J8/J5 between 0.34-0.66mm from edge
findings.detail: H2 and H4 at 0.16mm edge clearance are borderline for standard fabrication (typical 0.2mm minimum). Connectors are intentionally close to edge for panel-mount access — this is expected for test fixture connectors.

findings.summary: PCB net count (282) substantially exceeds flattened schematic net count (187) due to hierarchical sheet expansion — expected for 4-DUT replicated hierarchy
findings.detail: The dut_hub schematic uses dut.kicad_sch instantiated 4 times. Each DUT instance adds unique net names in the flattened netlist. The schematic analyzer counts 187 nets in the top-level view; the PCB sees all 282 fully-qualified net names after hierarchy expansion.

findings.summary: KiCad 5 legacy .sch keyboard PCB: 175 components — 77 key switches (MX-NoLED), 69 diodes (1N4148W), 8 capacitors, 6 mounting holes, 6 resistors, 3 inductors, 2 ICs, 2 connectors, 1 crystal, 1 fuse
findings.detail: ATmega32U4-AU (U1) as controller, WE-TVS-82400102 (U2) as USB ESD protection, Crystal_GND24_Small (Y1) with 22pF load caps. Power rails: +5V, GND, VCC. One rotary encoder (RE0) and one push switch (SW1) outside the key matrix.

findings.summary: Three decorative graphic symbols misidentified as power rails: 'Logo_Open_Hardware_Small', 'OSHW-Text-Logo', 'mini-eLiXiVy-Logo' appear in statistics.power_rails
findings.detail: These are KiCad graphical symbols from 'Graphic:Logo_Open_Hardware_Small' and custom 'mini_random_library' libs. They have power-symbol shape but carry no electrical signal. They pollute the power_rails list alongside legitimate +5V, GND, VCC. The analyzer should filter symbols from libs containing 'Graphic' or with 'Logo' in lib_id.

findings.summary: Key matrix detection reports switches_on_matrix=130 and estimated_keys=130, but the actual matrix is 5 rows x 15 cols = 75 switches; there are only 75 MX-NoLED components in the BOM
findings.detail: The analyzer uses net_name detection and counts 130 switches on the matrix, significantly overcounting. The actual keyboard has 75 MX switch positions. The 69 diodes (one per key with one spare) confirm 75 is the correct key count. The overcounting likely arises from double-counting switch connections when multiple net segments are enumerated in the KiCad 5 legacy parser.

findings.summary: WE-TVS-82400102 (U2) correctly detected as ESD IC protecting USB_D+, USB_D-, MCU_D+, MCU_D- — full USB signal chain protected
findings.detail: Protection device covers both USB connector side and MCU side differential pairs. Fuse F1 (500mA) on VCC rail from +5V correctly detected. USB ESD observation notes has_esd_protection=false for USB_D- which is a false negative since U2 explicitly lists USB_D- in protected_nets.

findings.summary: 302x93mm 2-layer keyboard PCB, 175 footprints (155 front / 20 back), 77 THT switch positions, standard DFM except board size exceeding 100x100mm
findings.detail: 77 THT components match the 77 switch-type components (75 MX + RE0 + SW1). SMD components (92) are controller, diodes, passives. Single ground domain with GND pour on both layers. One edge clearance warning for J1 at -1.55mm (component overlaps board edge — likely intentional for USB connector overhang).

findings.summary: Gerber set complete for 2-layer board: 7 gerber files (F.Cu, B.Cu, F.Mask, B.Mask, F.SilkS, B.SilkS, Edge.Cuts) plus PTH and NPTH drill files. Only missing recommended F.Paste.
findings.detail: Board dimensions 302.21x93.47mm confirmed in Edge.Cuts. 583 total holes: 29 vias (0.399mm drill) + 301 component holes (0.701mm MX key stems) + 253 mounting holes including 150 switch stabilizer holes (1.47mm PTH) and 85 switch mounting holes (3.99mm NPTH). rev.1 has 2 more holes than rev.1.1 (583 vs 581).

findings.summary: rev.1.1 gerber set identical layer structure to rev.1 with 2 fewer vias (27 vs 29) and identical flashes/draws counts
findings.detail: Same board dimensions, same component hole count (301), same mounting hole count (253). Via count reduced from 29 to 27 between revisions. Both revisions missing recommended F.Paste layer.

findings.summary: NRF52840-based IoT board: 78 total components — 28 capacitors, 15 resistors, 7 inductors (2 real + 5 LED misclassifications), 6 ICs, 5 switches, 4 LEDs (WS2812), 4 mounting holes (HDR type), 2 transistors, 2 ferrite beads, 2 diodes, 1 relay (tactile switch), 1 fuse
findings.detail: Main MCU: NRF52840-QIAA-R (U1). LDO: XC6206P332MR (U4, VBUSnRF->+3V3). Humidity sensor: SHT40-CD1B-R3 (U5). USB ESD: PRTR5V0U2X (D0). Antenna: ANT3216LL00R2400A (U6). 32MHz crystal X2 misclassified as connector (EASYEDA lib). Power rails: +3V3, GND, VBUS.

findings.summary: 32MHz crystal X2 (Q22FA12800025, EPSON FA-128) classified as 'connector' type instead of 'crystal', causing it to be absent from crystal_circuits detection
findings.detail: X2 uses lib_id 'EASYEDA:Q22FA12800025' with footprint 'easyeda2kicad:CRYSTAL-SMD_4P-L2.0-W1.6-BL'. The 4-pin SMD crystal footprint and CRYSTAL-SMD keyword in footprint name are not being used to override the lib-based type classification. The crystal_circuits section is empty despite the 32MHz crystal being essential for NRF52840 USB operation. The analyzer should check footprint name for CRYSTAL keyword as a classification hint.

findings.summary: Five 0603 white LEDs (LD1-LD5, value '0603White light_C2290') misclassified as 'inductor' type; one tactile switch KEY1 (TS-1003S-07026) misclassified as 'relay'
findings.detail: LD1-LD5 footprint is 'easyeda2kicad:LED0603-R-RD_WHITE' and datasheet links to an LED product page; the LCSC C2290 part is a white LED. These are classified as inductor because the lib_id or value heuristic matches inductor patterns. KEY1 is XUNPU TS-1003S-07026, a 12x12mm tactile switch (not a relay). Correct statistics should be: led=9, switch=6, inductor=2, relay=0.

findings.summary: NRF52840 RF matching network (L1=4.7nH, L2=2.2nH, C1=1.0pF, C2=1.2pF) detected as lc_filters at 2.1-3.1GHz but not as rf_matching or rf_chains
findings.detail: Three LC filter combinations are detected with resonant frequencies 2.12GHz, 2.32GHz, 3.10GHz — all in the 2.4GHz BLE band. Antenna U6 (ANT3216LL00R2400A) is present in a subcircuit with L2 neighbor. The rf_matching and rf_chains detectors both return empty. This is a missed detection: the 2.4GHz LC network around a BLE antenna should classify as rf_matching.

findings.summary: LDO XC6206P332MR correctly detected (VBUS->VBUSnRF->+3V3 path), WS2812 4-LED addressable chain detected, PRTR5V0U2X USB ESD protection detected
findings.detail: LDO topology correctly identified with input rail VBUSnRF and output +3V3. LED chain D1-D4 is WS2812E-V5, 240mA estimated current (60mA per LED). I2C bus SDA_qwIIC/SCL_qwIIC present for Qwiic connector (X1). SWD debug port SWD1 present. USB_GND and GND are correctly identified as separate ground domains (USB isolation with ferrite L4).

findings.summary: Q2 transistor circuit incorrectly lists U4 (XC6206 LDO), U5 (SHT40 sensor) as gate_driver_ics because its gate is tied to +3V3 power rail
findings.detail: Q2 (2N7002) is used as a level converter with gate permanently tied to +3V3. The analyzer detects any IC connected to the +3V3 net as a potential gate driver, including the LDO and humidity sensor. True gate driver is the NRF52840 GPIO. The gate_net='+3V3' correctly shows the gate is tied to power, but the driver inference is misleading.

findings.summary: 64.95x54.0mm 2-layer board, 85 footprints (84 front/1 back), DFM tier 'challenging' due to 0.1mm tracks and 0.075mm annular ring below advanced process minimum
findings.detail: NRF52840 thermal pad (4.85x4.85mm, 23.52mm2) with VSS_PA net has 29 nearby thermal vias including 25 footprint-defined via pads and 4 standalone vias. Zone stitching on VSS_PA covers both layers (4868mm2 area). Single-side assembly (84/85 components on front). USB_GND is a separate ground domain with 4 components (L4, R1, R2, USBC1) — correct USB isolation.

findings.summary: DFM violations: 0.1mm track width requires advanced process (standard min 0.127mm) and 0.075mm annular ring is below advanced process minimum (0.1mm)
findings.detail: The 0.075mm annular ring is classified as 'challenging' tier — below even JLCPCB advanced process (0.1mm). This likely requires a specialty fab or was an oversight. The 0.1mm track width is tight but achievable with advanced fab. Board size (64.95x54.0mm) fits within standard tier.
